name: Rengar

basic_attack.calculation_notes: Basic-attack timing values are sourced from From Online stats payload.

passive.name: Unseen Predator
passive.description_source: Innate:  Rengar generates Ferocity when he casts a basic ability or uses  Savagery's empowered attack, which expires after a period out of combat. At max Ferocity, his next basic ability consumes them to become empowered with an additional effect, gain an extra cast, and briefly grant him  bonus movement speed.

effects.description: Innate: Upon casting a basic ability or using Savagery's first attack, Rengar generates a stack of Ferocity for 1.5 seconds, refreshing on subsequent triggers and stacking up 4 times. At maximum stacks, his next basic ability consumes them all to become empowered with an additional effect and grant him an additional cast along with 30% / 40% / 50% (based on level) bonus movement speed for 1.5 seconds. Generated Ferocity is prevented from expiring for 10 seconds after entering champion combat.Reaching 4 Ferocity with Savagery or Bola Strike locks the empowered version of the ability from being cast for 0.1 seconds. Similarly, consuming all Ferocity with an empowered ability locks the unempowered version of the ability from being cast for 0.1 seconds if it is not already on cooldown, increased to 0.25 seconds for Battle Roar.
Innate: While in brush, Rengar gains Unseen Predator, which lingers for 0.35 seconds upon exiting brush. Unseen Predator is lost immediately if Rengar exits the brush using Flash or Hexflash.

Unseen Predator: Rengar's basic attacks have massively increased range and cause him to leap to the target's location if they are beyond his normal basic attack range, triggering on-attack effects, and attacking the target upon arrival and landing closer than his normal melee range. Unseen Predator will generate 1 Ferocity if none of his current Ferocity was generated this way.Rengar can cast any of his abilities during the dash.

Innate - Bonetooth Necklace: Scoring a takedown against an enemy champion within 3 seconds of damaging them grants Rengar a Trophy, once per unique champion and up to 5. An additional Trophy is made available through the event The Hunt is On! with the Head of Kha'Zix. Trophies grant bonus attack damage equal to 1% : 36% (based on Trophies) bonus AD.

passive.description: Innate: Upon casting a basic ability or using Savagery's first attack, Rengar generates a stack of Ferocity for 1.5 seconds, refreshing on subsequent triggers and stacking up 4 times. At maximum stacks, his next basic ability consumes them all to become empowered with an additional effect and grant him an additional cast along with 30% / 40% / 50% (based on level) bonus movement speed for 1.5 seconds. Generated Ferocity is prevented from expiring for 10 seconds after entering champion combat.Reaching 4 Ferocity with Savagery or Bola Strike locks the empowered version of the ability from being cast for 0.1 seconds. Similarly, consuming all Ferocity with an empowered ability locks the unempowered version of the ability from being cast for 0.1 seconds if it is not already on cooldown, increased to 0.25 seconds for Battle Roar.
context_notes: Source notes (summary): The start of the leap is considered to be the end of Rengar's attack windup, triggering attack effects (i.

basic_ability_1.name: Savagery
basic_ability_1.description_source: Active:  Rengar gains  bonus attack speed for his next two  basic attacks. His next basic attack deals bonus physical damage and will  critically strike, dealing increased damage based on his  critical strike chance.

effects.description: Active: Rengar gains 40% bonus attack speed on his next two basic attacks within 3 seconds.
Bonus Physical Damage
effects.context_notes: His first attack is empowered to stab the target, having an uncancellable windup, gaining 25 bonus range, and dealing bonus physical damage.

effects.description: Ferocity Bonus: Savagery's damage is modified to deal 30 : 255 (based on level) (+ 30% AD) bonus physical damage. Rengar instead gains 50% : 107% (based on level) bonus attack speed for 5 seconds.
Savagery resets Rengar's basic attack timer.

basic_ability_1.description: Active: Rengar gains 40% bonus attack speed on his next two basic attacks within 3 seconds.
context_notes: Source notes (summary): Savagery deals basic damage, but also triggers spell effects by also being tagged as spell damage.
Manual review (2026-02-24): Savagery is cast-gated and attack-cadence-coupled; the cast arms empowered strikes, while the bonus-damage packet resolves on empowered-hit contact.
Manual review (2026-02-24): The first empowered strike is the critical-strike-enabled stab branch and the attack-timer reset allows immediate weave into that empowered-hit window.

basic_ability_2.name: Battle Roar
basic_ability_2.description_source: Passive:  Rengar stores grey health equal to a portion of damage taken within a brief moment.

effects.description: Passive: Rengar stores 50% of the post-mitigation damage he has taken in the last 1.5 seconds as grey health on his health bar, increased to 100% of damage taken from monsters.
Magic Damage
effects.context_notes: Active: Rengar roars, dealing magic damage to nearby enemies and 65 : 137.

effects.description: Ferocity Bonus: Battle Roar's damage is modified to deal 50 : 240 (based on level) (+ 80% AP) magic damage. Rengar cleanses himself from all crowd control.

basic_ability_2.description: Passive: Rengar stores 50% of the post-mitigation damage he has taken in the last 1.5 seconds as grey health on his health bar, increased to 100% of damage taken from monsters.
context_notes: Source notes (summary): Grey health will not be consumed upon casting Battle Roar despite visually appearing so.

basic_ability_3.name: Bola Strike
basic_ability_3.description_source: Active:  Rengar throws a bola in the target direction that deals  physical damage and briefly  slows the first enemy hit,  revealing them for a short time.

effects.description: Physical Damage
effects.context_notes: Active: Rengar throws a bola in the target direction that deals physical damage to the first enemy hit and slows them for 1.75 seconds, as well as revealing them for 2 seconds and granting sight of a 150-unit radius around them for the same duration.

effects.description: Slow
effects.context_notes: Active: Rengar throws a bola in the target direction that deals physical damage to the first enemy hit and slows them for 1.75 seconds, as well as revealing them for 2 seconds and granting sight of a 150-unit radius around them for the same duration.

effects.description: Ferocity Bonus: Bola Strike's damage is modified to deal 50 : 335 (based on level) (+ 80% bonus AD) physical damage. The target is rooted instead of slowed.

basic_ability_3.description: Active: Rengar throws a bola in the target direction that deals physical damage to the first enemy hit and slows them for 1.75 seconds, as well as revealing them for 2 seconds and granting sight of a 150-unit radius around them for the same duration.
context_notes: Source notes (summary): If Rengar casts Savagery and/or Battle Roar during Bola Strike's cast time, he will buffer them and cast them when Bola Strike completes.

ultimate.name: Thrill of the Hunt
ultimate.description_source: Passive:  Rengar also gains  Unseen Predator while  camouflaged.

effects.description: Passive: Rengar also gains Unseen Predator while camouflaged from any source.
Effect Duration
effects.context_notes: Active: Rengar prowls for a duration, gaining bonus movement speed.

effects.description: Bonus Movement Speed
effects.context_notes: Active: Rengar prowls for a duration, gaining bonus movement speed.

effects.description: During this time, Rengar will detect the nearest enemy champion in range, revealing and gaining sight of a 100-unit radius around them. Enemies detected are revealed for the remaining full duration.
The nearest detected enemy within 1600 range is also marked, alerting all of their allies within 1200 range about Rengar's presence.
Armor Reduction
effects.context_notes: Rengar's next basic attack within 125 range against any enemy or with Unseen Predator to the marked enemy deals 100% AD bonus physical damage, then inflicts armor reduction for 4 seconds.

effects.description: Attacking or casting abilities other than Savagery ends Thrill of the Hunt immediately.

ultimate.description: Passive: Rengar also gains Unseen Predator while camouflaged from any source.
context_notes: Source notes (summary): Thrill of the Hunt's duration starts on-cast.

sources.used_for: canonical champion baseline generation for stats, abilities, and execution metadata
manual verification of ability timing and execution semantics for context-note normalization

sources.type: League of Legends Wiki (ability data template)
sources.used_for: manual behavior verification for Savagery empowered-hit cadence, attack-reset timing, and critical-first-hit branch resolution

notes: Generated from From Online champion payload as part of champion corpus parity coverage.
Ability execution metadata is source-derived from castTime/speed/effectRadius when present.
This baseline should be manually refined for high-impact simulation nuances before runtime scripting expansion.

schema_notes.base_by_rank: Arrays indexed by ability rank (1-based in game, 0-based in data).
schema_notes.base_by_champion_level: Arrays indexed by champion level minus one.
schema_notes.formula: Formula objects use explicit op/terms/output_stat when reliable source math is available.
schema_notes.ratio_type: Common ratio types: total_ad, bonus_ad, ap, target_max_health, target_missing_health.
schema_notes.value_percent: Percent values are represented as whole percentages unless noted otherwise.
schema_notes.basic_attack: Basic attack timing and projectile defaults are source-derived with documented fallbacks.
schema_notes.basic_attack_raw_timing_stats: Raw timing stats preserve source payload fields for auditability.
